content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 25, 1993

Claudia Coleman
Rees & Associates Inc.
1121 L Street, Suite 808
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-18

Dear Ms. Coleman:

You have requested confirmation of advice provided to you at
an FPPC workshop concerning the lobbying provisions of the
Political Reform Act ("Act").1/ Because you have not identified
the person on whose behalf you are seeking advice, we treat your
letter as a request for informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

Does a lobbyist need to attend a lobbyist ethics course
during the 1993-94 Legislative Session if the lobbyist attended
the course on December 13, 1990 and executed his/her Lobbyist
Certification Form (Form 604) for the 1993-94 Legislative Session
on December 1, 1992?

CONCLUSION

The lobbyist would not be required to take the ethics course
during the 1993-94 Legislative Session.

___________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

T-93-18
Page #


ANALYSIS

In 1990 legislation was enacted to require lobbyists to
attend an orientation course on the relevant ethical issues and
laws relating to lobbying. (Chapter 84, Stats. 1990.) Upon
filing a lobbyist certification a lobbyist must indicate whether
he/she has taken the course within the previous 24 months.
(Section 86103(d)(1).) If more than 24 months has elapsed, the
lobbyist must attend a course within a reasonable period of time
and his/her certification is accepted on a conditional basis until
the course is taken. (Section 86103(2).) According to your
facts, when the lobbyist signed the verification section of the
Form 604 on December 1, 1992, he/she had taken the course within
the previous 24 months. Therefore, the lobbyist is not required
to take the course for the 1993-94 Legislation Session.37

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant


37     Legislation may be proposed in the 1993-94 Legislative
Session to clarify this requirement for future registrations.
